hooks.command: cmd=$(jq -r '.tool_input.command // ""'); if echo "$cmd" | grep -Eq '(^|[ \t;&|`$(])git[ \t]+(checkout|restore)([ \t]|$)|git[ \t]+reset[ \t]+(--hard|--keep)([ \t]|$)|git[ \t]+clean[ \t]+(-[a-eg-z]*f|-f[a-z]*)([ \t]|$)'; then printf '{"hookSpecificOutput":{"hookEventName":"PreToolUse","permissionDecision":"deny","permissionDecisionReason":"Destructive git command blocked by .claude/settings.json hook. This command can silently overwrite uncommitted working-tree changes. Stash dirty work first (git stash push -u), then ASK THE USER before proceeding. See ~/.claude/projects/-Users-wurp-projects-ourcloud/memory/feedback_destructive_git.md for the recovery-options-before-checkout rule."}}'; fi; exit 0
